title: hypericum empetrifolium subsp. tortuosum
intro: Hypericum empetrifolium subsp. tortuosum, also known as St. John's Wort or Klamath weed, is a flowering plant that belongs to the St. John's Wort family and is native to the western United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Hypericum empetrifolium subsp. tortuosum include its ability to reduce anxiety and depression, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system, as well as its potential to treat wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
